items.title: De Zerbi vows Spurs can win last 5 to survive
items.summary: Roberto de Zerbi believes Tottenham can win all five remaining Premier League games to emphatically avoid relegation -- and warned any unhappy players will be turned away from the club's training ground on Monday.

items.title: What to watch for in Premier League title showdown
items.summary: We still have a month to go in the Premier League, but it feels like Arsenal's visit to Manchester City on Sunday will be the match that decides the destination of the title.

items.title: Rosenior: Estêvão in tears after 'devastating' injury
items.summary: Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior has revealed that Estêvão was in tears at half-time after he was forced off with an injury early in his side's defeat to Manchester United on Sunday.

items.title: 'PSG laat oog vallen op aanvallers van RB Leipzig en Arsenal'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Nieuwe energie bij de Oranje Leeuwinnen: 'Er is iets ontstaan'
items.summary: De sfeer bij de Oranje Leeuwinnen is weer positief en directe kwalificatie voor het WK 2027 in Brazilië ligt binnen handbereik. Verslaggever Chris Tempelman bespreekt in VI ZSM de nieuwe situatie onder bondscoach Arjan Veurink. 'Er is deze week iets ontstaan bij Oranje.'

items.title: Manchester City v Arsenal, the Merseyside derby, and more – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ Today’s games | Latest tables | Premier League top scorers ⚽ Follow us on Bluesky | And you can get in touch with Tom OK, so it was all building to this, then. The slow‑burn plotlines. The room‑temperature action sequences. The winter afternoons on the sofa watching men wrestle unhappily, staring out of the window as the frigid wind tousles the clouds, wondering about the death of all things, and also why referees not only have to speak now but speak in the same awkward Yorkshire bingo‑caller voice. Continue reading...

items.title: Un jeune talent du RSC Anderlecht serait proche de signer son premier contrat professionnel
items.summary: Selon les informations d'Africafoot, le jeune Saliou Dia devrait signer son premier contrat au RSC Anderlecht.

items.title: Derby tussen Charleroi en Standard eindigt in chaos na acties van Epolo: coaches zijn duidelijk over situatie
items.summary: Het werd even heet na het laatste fluitsignaal bij Charleroi-Standard, nadat Matthieu Epolo de Storm Ultras had uitgedaagd. Vincent Euvrard en Mario Kohnen gaven daar op de persconferentie allebei hun kijk op.

items.title: Harde hand bij Spurs: 'Als je gaat treuren, ga je naar huis'
items.summary: Roberto De Zerbi wil in de laatste weken van het seizoen alle neuzen dezelfde kant op bij Tottenham Hotspur. De positieve kant, welteverstaan. Voor sippe gezichten is even geen plaats op het trainingscomplex van Spurs. Want: 'We kunnen gewoon nog vijf wedstrijden op rij winnen.'

items.title: Doelpunt in 99ste minuut leidt tot droomfinale om League Two-ticket
items.summary: Engeland maakt zich op voor een zinderende ontknoping. Emmanuel Dieseruvwe schoot Rochdale, de runner-up van de National League, zaterdag in de 99ste minuut naar een cruciale overwinning op bezoek bij Braintree Town. Dat betekent dat de club volgende week in een rechtstreeks duel met York City uitmaakt wie terugkeert in het Engelse profvoetbal.

items.title: Bekerfinale Live: AZ en NEC strijden om Dennenappel én Europa League-ticket
items.summary: Het aftellen is begonnen! AZ en NEC staan om 18.00 uur tegenover elkaar in de finale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. Wie gaat er met de Dennenappel én het Europese ticket vandoor? VI houdt je op de hoogte van de laatste ontwikkelingen.

items.title: Un visiteur très spécial pour Nathan Saliba, et une bonne nouvelle pour Anderlecht ?
items.summary: Nathan Saliba a reçu la visite de son sélectionneur, Jesse Marsch, Derrière les Casernes ce samedi.

items.title: Rode Duivel Senne Lammens is bijzonder eerlijk na hold-up van United
items.summary: Manchester United heeft zaterdag met veel meeval gewonnen op het veld van Chelsea. De Londenaars domineerden bijna de hele wedstrijd, maar botsten op een stevig blok én een sterke Senne Lammens.

items.title: Club of Union? Boskamp ziet duidelijke titelfavoriet: "Daarom zie ik ze kampioen worden"
items.summary: Union SG en Club Brugge lijken onder elkaar uit te maken wie straks kampioen wordt. Voor hun onderlinge topper in het Dudenpark ziet Johan Boskamp voorlopig nog altijd één ploeg als de meest stabiele titelfavoriet.

items.title: Une implication réelle : N'golo Kanté a passé un coup de fil aux joueurs de Virton !
items.summary: La présence de N'golo Kanté à l'Excelsior Virton peut parfois sembler un peu "abstraite". Mais le champion du monde 2018 est bel et bien impliqué dans le club gaumais.

items.title: Nathan Saliba krijgt opvallend bezoek: ook heel goed nieuws voor Anderlecht
items.summary: Nathan Saliba kreeg zaterdag opvallend bezoek in Mechelen. De middenvelder van Anderlecht speelde onder het toeziend oog van de Canadese bondscoach Jesse Marsch, die hem na afloop ook nog persoonlijk aansprak.

items.title: Matthieu Epolo a presque initié une bagarre générale au Mambourg : "Charleroi a mis de l'huile sur le feu"
items.summary: C'était très tendu au coup de sifflet final de Charleroi - Standard, après le chambrage de Matthieu Epolo en direction des Storm Ultras. Un événement commenté par Vincent Euvrard et Mario Kohnen en conférence de presse.

items.title: Terrible nouvelle pour le Bayern : Vincent Kompany perd un cadre avant le PSG
items.summary: Serge Gnabry s'est blessé et sera absent plusieurs semaines. L'attaquant du Bayern manquera la demi-finale de Ligue des Champions contre le PSG.

items.title: Marc Degryse ziet één groot verschil tussen Club Brugge en Union
items.summary: Club Brugge staat zondagavond voor een cruciale topper op het veld van Union SG. Marc Degryse ziet één duidelijk voordeel bij de Brusselaars en legt daarmee meteen een gevoelig werkpunt bloot bij blauw-zwart.

items.title: How will Arteta set Arsenal up for Man City showdown?
items.summary: Novel solutions, no matter how small, might decide the Premier League title. We take a closer look at how Arsenal have played recently and what this means for the run-in.

items.title: Messi stunner gives Miami's new coach debut win
items.summary: Lionel Messi had two goals, including a stunning 79th-minute winner, to give Inter Miami a victory in new interim coach Guillermo Hoyos' first match in charge Saturday.

items.title: Matarazzo's Copa win makes U.S. coaching history
items.summary: Real Sociedad's Copa del Rey win made Pellegrino Matarazzo the first U.S.-born coach to win a trophy in Europe's "big five" leagues.

items.title: Carrick silences doubters as Man Utd close on Champions League
items.summary: Manchester United beat Chelsea to move closer to a Champions League return and silence the growing Michael Carrick doubters.

items.title: Protests and another costly loss - pressure mounts on Rosenior's Chelsea
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior admits his side face a "mountain to climb" as their campaign unravels at a crucial juncture following defeat by Manchester United.

items.title: Under-fire Liam Rosenior admits Chelsea have ‘a mountain to climb’
items.summary: Pressure grows after defeat by Manchester United ‘People don’t want to hear me say how well we played’ Liam Rosenior admitted that Chelsea have a mountain to climb after a 1-0 defeat by Manchester United put another dent in their hopes of qualifying for the Champions League. Matheus Cunha scored the only goal as United tightened their grip on third place by overcoming their defensive problems at Stamford Bridge, heaping the pressure on Rosenior after a dismal run. Continue reading...

items.title: Real Sociedad lift Copa del Rey after Marrero shootout heroics sink Atlético
items.summary: Atlético Madrid 2-2 Real Sociedad (aet: 3-4 on pens) Lookman 19, Alvarez 83; Barrenetxea 1, Oyarzabal 45+1pen History has a pair unexpected heroes. Unai Marrero, a 24-year-old backup goalkeeper, born in San Sebastián and raised at Real Sociedad, saved two penalties in the shootout to put his boyhood club within a single shot of victory on the what his captain had called the night of their lives. Then he embraced Pablo Marin, the former ballboy who now walked towards him carrying all of their hopes on his shoulders, kissed him on the cheek and asked his teammate to take them over the line. So Marin, 22, and on as a substitute, did just that, stepping up and securing only the fourth Copa del Rey in la Real’s history, defeating Atlético Madrid from the spot. Last time they had won it, in 2021, it took a penalty. This time it took six of them; Mikel Oyarzabal, as he had done then, scored one during the 90 minutes on the way to a 2-2 draw and three more men did in the shootout. Back then, Real Sociedad had won the trophy it in an empty stadium, unable to avoid the feeling that something was missing. Now at last they had done it in front of thousands of fans in Seville – there to see a trophy lifted for the first time in 38 years. Continue reading...

items.title: Wild celebrations to verge of tears - time running out for Spurs
items.summary: Scenes of celebration turned to bitter disappointment as Tottenham's hopes of avoiding relegation from the Premier League were dealt a devastating blow against Brighton.

items.title: How Hampton's heroics bailed out Lionesses in win against Iceland
items.summary: Goalkeeper Hannah Hampton says she just "did my job" in pulling off three crucial late saves as England held on to beat Iceland 1-0.

items.title: ‘It’s not finished yet’: De Zerbi determined to stay positive despite Brighton body blow
items.summary: Spurs denied by late equaliser from Georginio Rutter ‘They have to come to the training ground with a smile’ Roberto De Zerbi has warned he has “no time to see negative people” among his Tottenham players after they were denied the chance to climb out of the relegation zone by an injury-time equaliser from his former club Brighton. Georginio Rutter’s strike made it 15 games without a victory for Spurs after they had twice led, first through Pedro Porro then Xavi Simons. The draw left Tottenham in the bottom three and a point behind West Ham who travel to Crystal Palace on Monday night. Nottingham Forest, one point ahead of the Hammers, host Burnley on Sunday. Continue reading...

items.title: England do just enough as Russo seals Women’s World Cup qualifying win over Iceland
items.summary: Iceland 0-1 England Russo goal and Hampton saves keep Lionesses top of group Snow-capped mountains provided an idyllic backdrop to a less impressive performance, but England’s 1-0 win over Iceland ensured they maintained a three-point gap over Spain in their bid to earn an automatic place at the 2027 World Cup. Only the four League A teams who top their groups will avoid the playoffs and qualify automatically, and with the European and world champions drawn in the same group, one will be left frustrated and with more games required to book their trip to Brazil. Continue reading...
